intro: Parosela tinctoria, also known as "African Bloodroot," is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to tropical Africa, used as a natural dye and in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Parosela tinctoria include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal and dye properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Parosela tinctoria include infusions, decoctions, and powder, often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions in traditional medicine.
